decision_value: A directly verified SAT extension settles C(12,6,4)=40; replayed UNSAT excludes one newly validated link orbit and determines whether frontier work remains relevant.
naive_cost: Returning immediately to the 12-node exclusion hard tail could spend at least 720 solver-seconds without testing whether either new orbit supplies a decisive positive 40-cover.
opportunities_considered: Exact fixed-link residual extension for each of the two new orbits.
A stratified three-node nine-orbit frontier discriminator.
chosen_reductions: Condition on each independently validated 20-block point link, leaving only the exact residual 20-block completion problem. Test exactly two representatives and stop immediately on SAT.
expected_throughput_gain: Two conditioned residual searches directly test theorem-settling witnesses in at most 600 solver-seconds and avoid unrelated frontier or generic local-search work.
soundness_basis: Both point links passed fresh direct validation and complete C2 wr S5 canonicalization. SAT is checked directly over all 495 quadruples; UNSAT requires independent CNF reconstruction and external DRAT replay before acceptance.
remains_uncompressed: The point-link catalogue is still not exhaustive, and the 12 genuine timeout leaves remain open if both fixed-link extensions are excluded.
fixed_protocol: Exactly orbit-8-t-16 then orbit-9-t-17, 300 seconds each, fixed residual sequential encoding; stop after SAT or two results.
hypothesis: At least one newly discovered point-link orbit may extend to a valid 40-block cover, a higher-value test than generic frontier or local search.
stop_rule: SAT stops immediately for direct validation; otherwise stop after both fixed-cap outcomes for independent replay review.
